new $40 gold pieces). 1t looks like
a cross between a beer check and

posed to it because it will not

they never saw two in such in
innate relation before.


Looking back through history,
from Pharaoh to Theodore, and
we find more trusts in the last -
years than ever before.


THE republicans declare they
want to restrict them. Are you 60
ing to commission the republicans
to murder their own children? To
slaughter their own brood?


DO you believe the farmer
should pay more for American
made goods than the Russian peas
ant along the Danube and the Ger
mans along the Rhine? Then you
are a republican.


The republicans promise to
regulate the tariff-regulate it up.
The democrats intend to regulate it
down.


When the republicans nomin-
ated Taft the ygave the kiss of
Judas. He is the father of that
kind of injunction which throws
the laboring man into dark,
dingy cell without indictment, trial
or conviction"


Judge Turner in his Introductory
remarks stigmatized the republi-
can powers as for the interest of
the dollar and not the man. He
said:


Decentralization has been pro
duced and corruption forces have
assailed the party in power. The
leadership of the republican party
has suffered from this cause. Con-
gress is nothing more than con-
servatory of aggregated wealth.
This apprehension is apparent to
all except those who. having ears
hear not, and eyes that see not.
Railroads are permitted to work
their wills unchecked. Coal, iron,
oil and timber is cornered by COM
bines and doled out at ever in
creasing prices. Wealth has piled
up until it is a positive menace to
our institutions.


THE people want return to
honest and correct manner of gov
eminent in the interest of the man
and not the dollar They will not
get it at the hands of the republi-
cans. The masses of the republi-
cans are honest and patriotic The
leaders are also honest and pa
triotic. One such leaders steps
have been opposed and thwarted
by his own party in his efforts to-
ward exposures of government
wrong by his own party.


'The prime need of the country
S democratic principles.


"A few days ago the change be-
gan back in Maine and | predict it
will sweep the whole country, Bry-
IN is the equal of the best man any
other party can produce


William Goodyear, democratic
nominee for congress, delivered a
strong address, following the close
Of Senator Cores speech
